seasons.notes: Northern CA varieties like Gravenstein arrive first in July. Fuji and Granny Smith peak in fall.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a plastic bag; keeps 1-2 months. Keep away from other produce — apples emit ethylene.

seasons.notes: Northern CA varieties like Gravenstein arrive first in July. Fuji and Granny Smith peak in fall.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a plastic bag; keeps 1-2 months. Keep away from other produce — apples emit ethylene.

seasons.notes: Northern CA varieties like Gravenstein arrive first in July. Fuji and Granny Smith peak in fall.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a plastic bag; keeps 1-2 months. Keep away from other produce — apples emit ethylene.

seasons.notes: Northern CA varieties like Gravenstein arrive first in July. Fuji and Granny Smith peak in fall.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a plastic bag; keeps 1-2 months. Keep away from other produce — apples emit ethylene.

seasons.notes: Blenheim/Royal is THE gold standard — complex sweet-tart, honeysuckle notes, Slow Food Ark of Taste. Season is often just 10 days in late June. Farmers market ONLY. Patterson dominates commercial — bred for machine harvest, not flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp, then refrigerate. Eat within 2-3 days of ripening.

seasons.notes: Castroville, CA is the artichoke capital of the world. Spring crop is larger and more tender than fall.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate unwashed in a plastic bag for up to a week. Sprinkle with water to keep fresh.

seasons.notes: Castroville, CA is the artichoke capital of the world. Spring crop is larger and more tender than fall.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate unwashed in a plastic bag for up to a week. Sprinkle with water to keep fresh.

seasons.notes: Available year-round in CA but peppery flavor is best in cool weather. Bolts and turns bitter in summer heat.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Best used within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Crisp like an apple, fragrant like a pear. Unlike European pears, these are eaten firm, not soft.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately — keeps up to 3 months in the crisper drawer.

seasons.notes: Crisp like an apple, fragrant like a pear. Unlike European pears, these are eaten firm, not soft.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately — keeps up to 3 months in the crisper drawer.

seasons.notes: CA Delta and Salinas Valley produce the best domestic asparagus. Thinner spears aren't younger — just from a younger plant.
seasons.storageTip: Stand upright in a jar of water in the fridge, like flowers. Use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: CA Delta and Salinas Valley produce the best domestic asparagus. Thinner spears aren't younger — just from a younger plant.
seasons.storageTip: Stand upright in a jar of water in the fridge, like flowers. Use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: CA Delta and Salinas Valley produce the best domestic asparagus. Thinner spears aren't younger — just from a younger plant.
seasons.storageTip: Stand upright in a jar of water in the fridge, like flowers. Use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: CA Delta and Salinas Valley produce the best domestic asparagus. Thinner spears aren't younger — just from a younger plant.
seasons.storageTip: Stand upright in a jar of water in the fridge, like flowers. Use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: CA Hass in Jun-Aug has 3x the oil/fat content of early-season fruit (6g/oz vs 2g/oz). The richness and creaminess come from fat that accumulates while hanging on the tree. Mexican imports fill shelves year-round.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Speed up in a paper bag with a banana. Refrigerate once ripe for 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: CA Hass in Jun-Aug has 3x the oil/fat content of early-season fruit (6g/oz vs 2g/oz). The richness and creaminess come from fat that accumulates while hanging on the tree. Mexican imports fill shelves year-round.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Speed up in a paper bag with a banana. Refrigerate once ripe for 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Mexican imports fill shelves year-round, peaking fall through spring. CA Hass in Jun-Aug has 3x the oil/fat content of early-season fruit.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Speed up in a paper bag with a banana. Refrigerate once ripe for 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Always imported, always available. No meaningful peak — global supply chain keeps shelves stocked.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp until ripe. Separate from the bunch to slow ripening. Fridge turns peel brown but fruit stays good.

seasons.notes: Always imported, always available. No meaningful peak — global supply chain keeps shelves stocked.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp until ripe. Separate from the bunch to slow ripening. Fridge turns peel brown but fruit stays good.

seasons.notes: Grown year-round in CA. Golden beets are milder than red. Don't toss the greens — they're delicious sautéed.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens immediately (they draw moisture). Roots keep 2-3 weeks in the fridge.

seasons.notes: Grown year-round in CA. Golden beets are milder than red. Don't toss the greens — they're delicious sautéed.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens immediately (they draw moisture). Roots keep 2-3 weeks in the fridge.

seasons.notes: Red, yellow, and orange are just riper green peppers — sweeter and more nutritious. CA grows huge volumes.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate whole and unwashed. Use within a week. Cut peppers last 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Red, yellow, and orange are just riper green peppers — sweeter and more nutritious. CA grows huge volumes.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate whole and unwashed. Use within a week. Cut peppers last 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Look for plump, deeply colored berries with no red drupelets. Red means underripe and sour.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until ready to eat. Refrigerate in a single layer on paper towels. Use within 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Look for plump, deeply colored berries with no red drupelets. Red means underripe and sour.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until ready to eat. Refrigerate in a single layer on paper towels. Use within 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Moro variety is the most common in CA — deep burgundy flesh with berry-like overtones. Color varies by cold exposure.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for up to 2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Moro variety is the most common in CA — deep burgundy flesh with berry-like overtones. Color varies by cold exposure.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for up to 2 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA coastal regions produce excellent berries. The white bloom on blueberries is natural and a sign of freshness.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate dry and unwashed. Wash just before eating. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: CA coastal regions produce excellent berries. The white bloom on blueberries is natural and a sign of freshness.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate dry and unwashed. Wash just before eating. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Counter-seasonal import. Quality is acceptable.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate dry and unwashed. Wash just before eating. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: CA coastal regions produce excellent berries. The white bloom on blueberries is natural and a sign of freshness.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate dry and unwashed. Wash just before eating. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Cool-weather crop that's sweeter after a light frost. Baby bok choy is more tender for quick stir-fries.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: CA grows 90%+ of US broccoli, mostly in Salinas Valley and Imperial Valley. Sweetest in cool months.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate dry and unwashed. Use within 5 days — yellowing florets mean it's past prime.

seasons.notes: CA grows 90%+ of US broccoli, mostly in Salinas Valley and Imperial Valley. Sweetest in cool months.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate dry and unwashed. Use within 5 days — yellowing florets mean it's past prime.

seasons.notes: Frost converts starches to sugars — winter sprouts are noticeably sweeter. Smaller ones are more tender.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate unwashed on the stalk if possible. Loose sprouts keep about a week.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA but best in cool months. Savoy cabbage is more delicate; napa is the go-to for Asian cooking.
seasons.storageTip: Tightly wrapped in the fridge, cabbage lasts 2+ weeks. Cut surfaces oxidize — use quickly once sliced.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA but best in cool months. Savoy cabbage is more delicate; napa is the go-to for Asian cooking.
seasons.storageTip: Tightly wrapped in the fridge, cabbage lasts 2+ weeks. Cut surfaces oxidize — use quickly once sliced.

seasons.notes: San Joaquin Valley is cantaloupe country. Ripe ones have a sweet floral scent at the stem end and a slight give.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Once ripe, refrigerate and eat within 3 days. Cut melon keeps 2 days covered in fridge.

seasons.notes: San Joaquin Valley is cantaloupe country. Ripe ones have a sweet floral scent at the stem end and a slight give.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Once ripe, refrigerate and eat within 3 days. Cut melon keeps 2 days covered in fridge.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA but cold weather concentrates sugars. Farmers market carrots in winter are a revelation.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens. Store in a bag in the fridge crisper for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA but cold weather concentrates sugars. Farmers market carrots in winter are a revelation.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens. Store in a bag in the fridge crisper for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA dominates US production. Purple and Romanesco varieties appear at farmers markets fall through spring.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a loosely sealed bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: CA dominates US production. Purple and Romanesco varieties appear at farmers markets fall through spring.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a loosely sealed bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: CA grows most US celery year-round. Inner stalks (the heart) are more tender and less fibrous.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in aluminum foil and refrigerate — keeps crisp for 2+ weeks.

seasons.notes: Rainbow chard is the same plant in different colors. Stems take longer to cook than leaves — start them first.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel in the fridge. Use within 5 days.

seasons.notes: Bing (late May-early Jun) is THE reference cherry. Rainier has highest sugar — premium pricing. Brooks (mid-Apr) is the earliest good cherry. CA season is only ~6 weeks.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately, unwashed, with stems on. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Bing (late May-early Jun) is THE reference cherry. Rainier has highest sugar — premium pricing. Brooks (mid-Apr) is the earliest good cherry. CA season is only ~6 weeks.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately, unwashed, with stems on. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Chilean cherries are surprisingly decent — cherries are hardier in cold storage than peaches. Worth buying in winter.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately, unwashed, with stems on. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Mark Twain called it the most delicious fruit known to man. Creamy custard texture with banana-pineapple flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp until slightly soft. Refrigerate once ripe and eat within 2 days.

seasons.notes: CA grows jalapeños, serranos, Anaheims, and more. Summer heat produces hotter peppers.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks. Freeze whole for long-term storage.

seasons.notes: CA grows jalapeños, serranos, Anaheims, and more. Summer heat produces hotter peppers.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks. Freeze whole for long-term storage.

seasons.notes: Like Brussels sprouts, sweetened by frost. Sturdier than kale — great for braising or as a wrap substitute.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a damp towel. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Sugar converts to starch rapidly after picking. Same-day corn from a farmers market is a completely different food.
seasons.storageTip: Eat the day you buy it. If you must store, keep in the husk in the fridge — use within 2 days.

seasons.notes: Persian cucumbers from CA are thinner-skinned and more flavorful than standard slicers. No need to peel.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap individually in paper towel and refrigerate. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Mexican imports fill the gap when CA production slows. Quality is good for slicers and Persians.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap individually in paper towel and refrigerate. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Medjool dates from the Coachella Valley are world-class. Fresh dates have a completely different caramel texture than dried.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a month, fridge for 6 months, freezer for a year.

seasons.notes: Red-fleshed varieties have more flavor than white. Look for bright, evenly colored skin without many brown spots.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate ripe fruit for up to 5 days. Cut dragon fruit oxidizes quickly — eat immediately.

seasons.notes: Red-fleshed varieties have more flavor than white. Look for bright, evenly colored skin without many brown spots.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate ripe fruit for up to 5 days. Cut dragon fruit oxidizes quickly — eat immediately.

seasons.notes: Japanese and Chinese varieties are more tender with fewer seeds than globe eggplant. Skin should be taut and glossy.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp if using within 2 days. Refrigerate loosely wrapped for up to a week.

seasons.notes: A spring farmers market staple. Double-shelling is tedious but worth it — blanch, then pop beans from their skins.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in pods. Use within a few days — they toughen quickly.

seasons.notes: A spring farmers market staple. Double-shelling is tedious but worth it — blanch, then pop beans from their skins.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in pods. Use within a few days — they toughen quickly.

seasons.notes: Anise flavor mellows dramatically when roasted. The fronds make an excellent garnish or salad addition.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate wrapped in a damp towel. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Two crops: a small breba crop in June, then the main crop Aug–Sept. Black Mission and Kadota are CA classics.
seasons.storageTip: Extremely perishable. Eat within 1-2 days. Handle gently — bruises quickly.

seasons.notes: Green garlic appears at farmers markets in spring. Fresh-harvest garlic in summer is juicier and milder than stored.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole bulbs in a cool, dry, dark place. Good for months. Don't refrigerate.

seasons.notes: CA Oro Blanco is sweeter and less bitter than standard varieties. Heavier fruit = juicier fruit.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA Oro Blanco is sweeter and less bitter than standard varieties. Heavier fruit = juicier fruit.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA produces 99% of US table grapes. New seedless varieties like Cotton Candy have transformed the category. Late-season Autumn Royal extends into January.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate unwashed in a ventilated bag. Wash just before eating.

seasons.notes: CA produces 99% of US table grapes. New seedless varieties like Cotton Candy have transformed the category.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate unwashed in a ventilated bag. Wash just before eating.

seasons.notes: French haricots verts are thinner and more tender. Beans should snap cleanly — if they bend, they're old.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within 5 days.

seasons.notes: Pink-fleshed varieties are sweeter. Ripe guavas smell powerfully fragrant from across the room.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate when ripe — use within 2 days.

seasons.notes: Pink-fleshed varieties are sweeter. Ripe guavas smell powerfully fragrant from across the room.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate when ripe — use within 2 days.

seasons.notes: Lacinato (Tuscan) kale is more tender than curly for salads. Frost-kissed winter kale is markedly sweeter.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag with a dry paper towel. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Lacinato (Tuscan) kale is more tender than curly for salads. Frost-kissed winter kale is markedly sweeter.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag with a dry paper towel. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: CA kiwis come from the Central Valley. Gold kiwi (usually imported) is sweeter with smooth edible skin.
seasons.storageTip: Firm kiwis ripen at room temp in a few days. Refrigerate ripe kiwis for up to 4 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA kiwis come from the Central Valley. Gold kiwi (usually imported) is sweeter with smooth edible skin.
seasons.storageTip: Firm kiwis ripen at room temp in a few days. Refrigerate ripe kiwis for up to 4 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA kiwis come from the Central Valley. Gold kiwi (usually imported) is sweeter with smooth edible skin.
seasons.storageTip: Firm kiwis ripen at room temp in a few days. Refrigerate ripe kiwis for up to 4 weeks.

seasons.notes: Eat whole — the sweet skin balances the tart flesh. Nagami variety is most common in CA.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a few days, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: The white and light green parts are what you eat. Split lengthwise and wash thoroughly — sand hides in the layers.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate unwashed. Keeps 1-2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Eureka lemons dominate CA production. Year-round availability but winter lemons are juicier and more aromatic.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for a month. Zest before juicing to get both.

seasons.notes: Eureka lemons dominate CA production. Year-round availability but winter lemons are juicier and more aromatic.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for a month. Zest before juicing to get both.

seasons.notes: A lemon-mandarin hybrid — sweeter, more floral, thinner-skinned. The signature CA citrus. Excellent for baking.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for up to a month.

seasons.notes: A lemon-mandarin hybrid — sweeter, more floral, thinner-skinned. The signature CA citrus. Excellent for baking.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for up to a month.

seasons.notes: Salinas Valley is America's Salad Bowl. Peak crispness in cooler months. Summer heat makes lettuce bolt and turn bitter.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in a damp paper towel and bag. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Nearly all US limes are imported from Mexico. Key limes are smaller, more aromatic, with more seeds.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks. Roll on counter before juicing for maximum yield.

seasons.notes: Often overlooked — grows abundantly in SoCal yards. Flavor is like a tangy apricot-plum. Brief farmers market season.
seasons.storageTip: Very perishable. Eat within 2-3 days. Do not stack — they bruise easily.

seasons.notes: Extremely short fresh season. The bumpy red shell peels easily to reveal translucent, perfumy flesh.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately. Keeps about a week. Shell darkens in fridge but fruit inside stays good.

seasons.notes: Extremely short fresh season. The bumpy red shell peels easily to reveal translucent, perfumy flesh.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately. Keeps about a week. Shell darkens in fridge but fruit inside stays good.

seasons.notes: Satsumas arrive first in November, followed by Clementines and Murcotts. CA mandarins are some of the world's best.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Satsumas arrive first in November, followed by Clementines and Murcotts. CA mandarins are some of the world's best.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: April-June Mexican Ataulfo is dramatically better than any other time/variety. Look for uniformly golden-yellow, slightly wrinkled fruit. Don't judge ripeness by color — judge by squeeze and aroma.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe for 3-5 days.

seasons.notes: Mexican Ataulfo (honey) mangoes are supremely creamy. Don't judge ripeness by color — judge by squeeze and aroma.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe for 3-5 days.

seasons.notes: Mexican Ataulfo (honey) mangoes are supremely creamy. Don't judge ripeness by color — judge by squeeze and aroma.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe for 3-5 days.

seasons.notes: Mexican Ataulfo (honey) mangoes are supremely creamy. Don't judge ripeness by color — judge by squeeze and aroma.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe for 3-5 days.

seasons.notes: Cultivated varieties (button, cremini, shiitake) are year-round. Wild chanterelles and porcini appear in fall/winter rains.
seasons.storageTip: Store in a paper bag in the fridge. Never seal in plastic — they get slimy.

seasons.notes: CA's flagship citrus. The navel is actually a conjoined twin orange. Best for eating; Valencia is better for juicing.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA's flagship citrus. The navel is actually a conjoined twin orange. Best for eating; Valencia is better for juicing.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: The juicing orange. Green patches on skin in summer are normal (regreening) and don't affect quality or flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for several weeks.

seasons.notes: The juicing orange. Green patches on skin in summer are normal (regreening) and don't affect quality or flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for several weeks.

seasons.notes: Snow Queen (late Jun) is the sweetest. Fantasia (late Jul) is the reference standard. Independence (mid-Jul) has rich tangy-sweet flavor. Arctic series (white, farmers markets) spans the whole season.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp stem-end down. Refrigerate once ripe — use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: Same cold-chain issues as imported peaches. Plums and cherries import better than nectarines.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp stem-end down. Refrigerate once ripe — use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: Spring onions at farmers markets are milder and sweeter. Stockton Red onions are a CA specialty in late spring.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole onions in a cool, dry, dark place. Cut onions: wrap tightly and fridge for a few days.

seasons.notes: Spring onions at farmers markets are milder and sweeter. Stockton Red onions are a CA specialty in late spring.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole onions in a cool, dry, dark place. Cut onions: wrap tightly and fridge for a few days.

seasons.notes: Maradol (Mexican) papayas are large with salmon-pink flesh. Solo papayas from Hawaii are smaller and sweeter.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp until skin is mostly yellow and fruit gives slightly. Refrigerate ripe for 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Maradol (Mexican) papayas are large with salmon-pink flesh. Solo papayas from Hawaii are smaller and sweeter.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp until skin is mostly yellow and fruit gives slightly. Refrigerate ripe for 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Needs frost to convert starch to sugar — winter parsnips are dramatically sweeter. Roast to caramelize.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Keeps 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Wrinkled skin means ripe, not old. The more shriveled, the sweeter. Intensely aromatic pulp with edible seeds.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp until wrinkled. Refrigerate ripe fruit for up to a week.

seasons.notes: Wrinkled skin means ripe, not old. The more shriveled, the sweeter. Intensely aromatic pulp with edible seeds.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp until wrinkled. Refrigerate ripe fruit for up to a week.

seasons.notes: Late July-early Aug is peak. O'Henry (early Aug) is the benchmark yellow peach. Elegant Lady (Jul) dominates grocery stores — bred for shipping, not flavor. Suncrest (mid-Jul, farmers market only) is legendary.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp stem-end down. Once ripe, refrigerate and eat within 3 days.

seasons.notes: Chilean peaches suffer from long cold chain + shipping varieties. Generally not worth buying — high mealiness risk.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp stem-end down. Once ripe, refrigerate and eat within 3 days.

seasons.notes: Pears ripen off the tree — buy firm and ripen at home. Bartlett changes color when ripe; Bosc and Anjou don't.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Check the neck — when it yields to gentle pressure, it's ready. Refrigerate ripe pears.

seasons.notes: Pears ripen off the tree — buy firm and ripen at home. Bartlett changes color when ripe; Bosc and Anjou don't.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Check the neck — when it yields to gentle pressure, it's ready. Refrigerate ripe pears.

seasons.notes: Sugar snaps are the total package — eat pod and all. English peas need shelling but have the sweetest flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately — sugars convert to starch fast. Best eaten same day.

seasons.notes: Sugar snaps are the total package — eat pod and all. English peas need shelling but have the sweetest flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate immediately — sugars convert to starch fast. Best eaten same day.

seasons.notes: Fuyu (squat) can be eaten firm like an apple. Hachiya (acorn-shaped) must be fully soft or it's painfully astringent.
seasons.storageTip: Fuyu: room temp until slightly soft. Hachiya: room temp until very soft and jelly-like.

seasons.notes: Year-round from Costa Rica but sweetest spring through summer. Pineapples don't ripen after picking — buy golden and fragrant.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp 1-2 days. Cut pineapple keeps 3-4 days refrigerated in an airtight container.

seasons.notes: Year-round from Costa Rica but sweetest spring through summer. Pineapples don't ripen after picking — buy golden and fragrant.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp 1-2 days. Cut pineapple keeps 3-4 days refrigerated in an airtight container.

seasons.notes: Santa Rosa (mid-Jun) is the iconic CA plum. Satsuma has blood-red flesh. Elephant Heart (late Jul, farmers market) is incredibly sweet but too soft to ship.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe — use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Chilean plums are more acceptable than imported peaches — plums handle the supply chain better.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe — use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Wonderful variety dominates CA production. Heavier = juicier. Score and break apart in water to avoid staining.
seasons.storageTip: Whole fruit keeps 1-2 months in fridge. Seeds keep a week refrigerated or freeze for months.

seasons.notes: The original citrus — grapefruit's ancestor. Milder and sweeter with thick pithy rind. Great in salads.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2 weeks.

seasons.notes: New potatoes (spring) have thin, flaky skin and creamy texture. Year-round from storage but spring digs are special.
seasons.storageTip: Cool, dark, dry place — never refrigerate (converts starch to sugar). Keep away from onions.

seasons.notes: Sugar pie pumpkins are for eating — those big jack-o'-lantern pumpkins are watery and flavorless.
seasons.storageTip: Whole pumpkins keep for months in a cool, dry place.

seasons.notes: Bitterness mellows dramatically when grilled or roasted. Treviso is the elongated variety — slightly less bitter.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate wrapped. Keeps about 2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Quick-growing spring crop. Watermelon radishes have stunning green-to-pink interiors. French breakfast radishes with butter and salt are perfect.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens. Submerge in water in the fridge for maximum crispness. Keeps 1-2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Quick-growing spring crop. Watermelon radishes have stunning green-to-pink interiors. French breakfast radishes with butter and salt are perfect.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens. Submerge in water in the fridge for maximum crispness. Keeps 1-2 weeks.

seasons.notes: CA coastal climate is ideal. Golden raspberries are milder and sweeter. Inspect containers for mold at the bottom.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until eating. Use within 2 days. Single-layer on paper towel in fridge.

seasons.notes: CA coastal climate is ideal. Golden raspberries are milder and sweeter. Inspect containers for mold at the bottom.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until eating. Use within 2 days. Single-layer on paper towel in fridge.

seasons.notes: CA coastal climate is ideal. Golden raspberries are milder and sweeter. Inspect containers for mold at the bottom.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until eating. Use within 2 days. Single-layer on paper towel in fridge.

seasons.notes: Only eat the stalks — leaves are toxic. Field-grown is tart and intense; hothouse is milder. Pairs perfectly with strawberries.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate wrapped in a damp towel. Keeps about 2 weeks. Freezes well chopped.

seasons.notes: Sweeter than turnips, especially after frost. Excellent mashed, roasted, or added to stews.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate. Keeps several weeks.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA but spring spinach is sweeter and more tender. Bolts in summer heat.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag with a paper towel. Use within 5 days.

seasons.notes: Smaller is better — large zucchini are watery and seedy. Blossoms are a delicacy, stuffed and fried.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Butternut, delicata, kabocha, acorn — each has distinct character. Delicata skin is edible. Flavor improves with storage.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole in a cool, dry place for months. Cut squash keeps a week in the fridge.

seasons.notes: CA grows 90% of US strawberries. Available almost year-round but spring berries are a different universe of flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until eating. Refrigerate on paper towels. Use within 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: CA grows 90% of US strawberries. Available almost year-round but spring berries are a different universe of flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Don't wash until eating. Refrigerate on paper towels. Use within 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Nutty, slightly sweet, with a crisp water chestnut texture when raw. Roast until caramelized for the best experience.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag. Keeps 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Garnet and Jewel varieties dominate CA. True yams are a different plant entirely — what Americans call yams are sweet potatoes.
seasons.storageTip: Store in a cool, dark place — never refrigerate raw (damages texture). Keeps several weeks.

seasons.notes: The papery husk peels off to reveal a sticky, tart fruit essential for salsa verde. Smaller ones tend to be sweeter.
seasons.storageTip: Leave husks on. Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: The papery husk peels off to reveal a sticky, tart fruit essential for salsa verde. Smaller ones tend to be sweeter.
seasons.storageTip: Leave husks on. Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Peak CA heirlooms in August are one of the great food experiences. Never refrigerate a tomato — it kills flavor and texture.
seasons.storageTip: Always room temp, stem-end down. Eat within a few days of ripening.

seasons.notes: Peak CA heirlooms in August are one of the great food experiences. Never refrigerate a tomato — it kills flavor and texture.
seasons.storageTip: Always room temp, stem-end down. Eat within a few days of ripening.

seasons.notes: Young turnips are mild and sweet. Hakurei (Japanese) turnips are small, white, and can be eaten raw like radishes.
seasons.storageTip: Remove greens. Refrigerate roots in a bag for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Look for a pronounced yellow ground spot and hollow sound when tapped. Seedless varieties dominate but seeded have more flavor. Imperial Valley starts May.
seasons.storageTip: Whole: room temp. Cut: refrigerate and eat within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Look for a pronounced yellow ground spot and hollow sound when tapped. Seedless varieties dominate but seeded have more flavor. Imperial Valley starts May.
seasons.storageTip: Whole: room temp. Cut: refrigerate and eat within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Vibrant magenta flesh, watermelon-bubblegum flavor. Handle with care — tiny invisible glochids are devilish.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate for up to a week. Use tongs or thick gloves when handling.

seasons.notes: Vibrant magenta flesh, watermelon-bubblegum flavor. Handle with care — tiny invisible glochids are devilish.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate for up to a week. Use tongs or thick gloves when handling.

seasons.notes: Minneola tangelos have a distinctive knob at the stem. Very juicy, tangy-sweet — a grapefruit-tangerine cross.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp for a week, fridge for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Tastes like a mild, sweet broccoli stem. Excellent raw, shredded into slaws, or roasted. Peel the tough outer skin.
seasons.storageTip: Remove leaves. Refrigerate for 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Nature's fractal — a psychedelic relative of cauliflower. Nuttier, more delicate flavor. Stunning in presentation.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Fresh olives are inedible raw — they must be cured. CA olive oil harvest peaks in fall. Mission and Manzanilla are key CA varieties.
seasons.storageTip: Process immediately if curing. Olive oil: store in dark, cool place.

seasons.notes: Fresh olives are inedible raw — they must be cured. CA olive oil harvest peaks in fall. Mission and Manzanilla are key CA varieties.
seasons.storageTip: Process immediately if curing. Olive oil: store in dark, cool place.

seasons.notes: Flavor King (mid-Aug) is widely considered THE best pluot — sweet, spicy, intensely complex. Dapple Dandy/Dinosaur Egg (mid-Aug to mid-Sep). Flavor Queen (early Jul) is honey-sweet. Pluots typically 2-5 more brix than plums. Created by Zaiger Genetics in Modesto, CA.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe — use within 3 days.

seasons.notes: Ugly but delicious — intense celery flavor. Classic in French rémoulade or pureed into soup.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Keeps 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Not actually broccoli — more related to turnips. Pleasantly bitter. Quick blanch before sautéing tames the bite.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a damp towel. Use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Often unfairly maligned — a good honeydew is floral and honey-sweet. Should feel slightly waxy, not rock-hard.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe. Cut melon keeps 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: Often unfairly maligned — a good honeydew is floral and honey-sweet. Should feel slightly waxy, not rock-hard.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp. Refrigerate once ripe. Cut melon keeps 2-3 days.

seasons.notes: CA produces 80% of the world's almonds. Fresh green almonds in spring are a brief farmers market delicacy — tart and crunchy.
seasons.storageTip: Fresh almonds: refrigerate, use within days. Dried: cool, dark place for months.

seasons.notes: Fresh-harvest walnuts in fall are milder and less bitter than stored. CA grows 99% of US walnuts.
seasons.storageTip: Shell and refrigerate or freeze. In-shell keeps months in a cool place.

seasons.notes: Fresh-harvest walnuts in fall are milder and less bitter than stored. CA grows 99% of US walnuts.
seasons.storageTip: Shell and refrigerate or freeze. In-shell keeps months in a cool place.

seasons.notes: Young garlic harvested before the bulb forms. Mild, green-onion-like with a gentle garlic warmth. A spring farmers market treasure.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Young garlic harvested before the bulb forms. Mild, green-onion-like with a gentle garlic warmth. A spring farmers market treasure.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Tangy, green-bean-like flavor. De-spine, slice, and grill or sauté until the slime cooks off. Essential in Mexican cuisine.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Tangy, green-bean-like flavor. De-spine, slice, and grill or sauté until the slime cooks off. Essential in Mexican cuisine.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Babcock (late Jun) is the classic. Snow series and Arctic whites at farmers markets. Sweeter, more floral than yellow. More delicate — buy at farmers markets.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp, then refrigerate. Eat within 2-3 days of ripening.

seasons.notes: Galaxy is the main variety. Flat shape, melting texture, very sweet. Increasingly available commercially.
seasons.storageTip: Ripen at room temp, then refrigerate. Eat within 2-3 days of ripening.

seasons.notes: Sugar converts to starch rapidly after picking. Same-day corn from a farmers market is a completely different food.
seasons.storageTip: Eat the day you buy it. If you must store, keep in the husk in the fridge — use within 2 days.

seasons.notes: Spring crop is sweeter. Eat pod and all — the snap is the whole point. Best raw or barely cooked.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Fall crop, slightly less sweet than spring but still excellent.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Flat pods, mild sweet flavor. Essential in stir-fries. De-string before cooking.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA, peak spring. Both white and green parts are usable. Grilled whole scallions are a revelation.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: The all-purpose cooking onion. Spring harvest is sweeter and juicier, fall storage onions are more pungent.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole in a cool, dry, dark place. Cut onions: wrap tightly and fridge for a few days.

seasons.notes: Imperial Valley's warm climate produces early-season onions.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole in a cool, dry, dark place. Cut onions: wrap tightly and fridge for a few days.

seasons.notes: Milder than yellow. Best raw in salads and sandwiches. Quick-pickle for tacos.
seasons.storageTip: Store whole in a cool, dry, dark place. Cut onions: wrap tightly and fridge for a few days.

seasons.notes: More complex than onion — sweet, slightly garlicky. Essential in vinaigrettes and French cooking.
seasons.storageTip: Store like onions in a cool, dry, dark place. Keeps for months.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA, best in cool months. The go-to for kimchi, dumplings, and Asian soups.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate wrapped. Keeps 1-2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Cool-season crop. Mild, juicy. Grate for condiment, braise, or pickle. Watermelon radishes have stunning interiors.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a bag. Keeps 2-3 weeks.

seasons.notes: Classic fall squash. Dark green skin, orange flesh. Roast halved with butter and brown sugar.
seasons.storageTip: Whole squash keeps for months in a cool dry place. Cut: refrigerate and use within a week.

seasons.notes: Classic fall squash. Dark green skin, orange flesh. Flavor improves with a few weeks of curing after harvest.
seasons.storageTip: Whole squash keeps for months in a cool dry place. Cut: refrigerate and use within a week.

seasons.notes: The edible-skin squash. Cream-colored with green stripes. Sweet, creamy, no peeling needed.
seasons.storageTip: Whole squash keeps for months in a cool dry place. Cut: refrigerate and use within a week.

seasons.notes: Japanese pumpkin. Dense, dry, intensely sweet flesh — like chestnut. Excellent roasted or in soup.
seasons.storageTip: Whole squash keeps for months in a cool dry place. Cut: refrigerate and use within a week.

seasons.notes: Smaller pods (under 3 inches) are more tender. High heat cooking (grilling, roasting, frying) reduces sliminess.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag. Use within 3 days — deteriorates quickly.

seasons.notes: Sungold is the standout — bursting sweet-tart flavor. Sweeter and more reliable than full-size tomatoes.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp always. Never refrigerate. Use within a few days.

seasons.notes: SoCal coastal climate extends the season. Dry-farmed cherry tomatoes have concentrated flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp always. Never refrigerate. Use within a few days.

seasons.notes: Slender, thin-skinned, fewer seeds than globe. Cooks faster, more tender. Excellent grilled whole.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp if using within 2 days. Refrigerate loosely wrapped for up to a week.

seasons.notes: Fresh limas are nothing like frozen — buttery, sweet, nutty. Christmas limas have stunning maroon markings.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in pods. Shell just before cooking. Use within a few days.

seasons.notes: Genovese is the standard for pesto. Thai basil holds up to heat better. Pinch flowers to extend harvest.
seasons.storageTip: Do not refrigerate — cold blackens leaves. Store stems in water on the counter.

seasons.notes: Genovese is the standard for pesto. Thai basil holds up to heat better. Pinch flowers to extend harvest.
seasons.storageTip: Do not refrigerate — cold blackens leaves. Store stems in water on the counter.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA. Cool-season herb — bolts quickly in heat. Fall crop is also excellent. Include stems in cooking — more flavor than leaves.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Use within 5-7 days.

seasons.notes: Flat-leaf (Italian) has more flavor. Curly is mainly for garnish. Much more than decoration — a key ingredient in chimichurri, tabbouleh, gremolata.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Keeps about a week.

seasons.notes: Truly year-round evergreen perennial. Grows like a weed on the CA coast. Woody stems make excellent skewers for grilling.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate for 2+ weeks. Dries easily.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA, most aromatic in warm months. Lemon thyme is a distinct variety with citrus notes.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Keeps about 2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Spearmint is milder, peppermint is more intense. Grows aggressively — if you plant it, contain it.
seasons.storageTip: Store stems in water on the counter or wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate.

seasons.notes: Fern-like fronds with anise-caraway flavor. Essential for pickles, salmon, potato salad. Flowers are edible too.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Use within a week — wilts fast.

seasons.notes: Year-round in CA. Mexican oregano is a different plant with a more robust flavor. Dried oregano is often more potent than fresh.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Dries very well.

seasons.notes: Velvety leaves, earthy-peppery flavor. Essential for brown butter sage pasta and Thanksgiving stuffing.
seasons.storageTip: Wrap in damp paper towel and refrigerate. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Cultivated year-round on supplemented sawdust logs. Rich umami flavor. Remove tough stems before cooking.
seasons.storageTip: Store in a paper bag in the fridge. Never seal in plastic — they get slimy. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Fast-growing cultivated variety. Delicate, velvety texture. Excellent seared at high heat until edges crisp.
seasons.storageTip: Store in a paper bag in the fridge. Very perishable — use within 3-4 days.

seasons.notes: Baby portobello. Cultivated year-round. More flavor than white button mushrooms. Versatile all-purpose mushroom.
seasons.storageTip: Store in a paper bag in the fridge. Never seal in plastic — they get slimy.

seasons.notes: Mature cremini. Cultivated year-round. Meaty texture makes it a popular burger substitute. Scrape out gills to reduce dark liquid.
seasons.storageTip: Store in a paper bag in the fridge. Use within 5-7 days.

seasons.notes: Hotter than jalapeño, bright flavor. The workhorse chile for fresh salsas. Summer heat produces spicier peppers.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks. Freeze whole for long-term storage.

seasons.notes: SoCal extends the pepper season. Smaller serranos tend to be hotter.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks. Freeze whole for long-term storage.

seasons.notes: Fruity, floral heat — far more complex than just hot. Orange variety is most common. Scotch bonnet is a close relative.
seasons.storageTip: Handle with gloves. Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks. Freeze for months.

seasons.notes: Mild, rich, and essential for chiles rellenos. When dried, it becomes ancho. Roast and peel for best flavor.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks.

seasons.notes: Mild, versatile chile named after Anaheim, CA. Excellent roasted. Very similar to New Mexico/Hatch chiles.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag for up to 2 weeks. Roast and freeze for year-round use.

seasons.notes: Small but extremely hot. Essential in Thai and Southeast Asian cooking. Both red and green are used.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate for up to 2 weeks. Dries and freezes well.

seasons.notes: Long, dark green chile. Mild to medium heat. When dried, becomes the pasilla. Used in mole sauces.
seasons.storageTip: Refrigerate in a paper bag. Use within a week.

seasons.notes: Always imported. Cook at all stages: green (starchy, like potato), yellow (sweet), black (very sweet for maduros).
seasons.storageTip: Room temp to ripen. Green plantains keep for weeks. Ripe ones refrigerate for a few days.

seasons.notes: Always imported. No meaningful seasonal quality variation.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp to ripen. Green plantains keep for weeks. Ripe ones refrigerate for a few days.

seasons.notes: Always imported. No meaningful seasonal quality variation.
seasons.storageTip: Room temp to ripen. Green plantains keep for weeks. Ripe ones refrigerate for a few days.

seasons.notes: Always imported. Young coconuts (white husk) have more water and soft jelly flesh. Brown coconuts have firmer meat.
seasons.storageTip: Whole coconuts keep for months. Once cracked

seasons.notes: Always imported. No meaningful seasonal quality variation.
seasons.storageTip: Whole coconuts keep for months. Once cracked

seasons.notes: Always imported. Look for firm, smooth skin — wrinkles mean old. Young ginger (pink tips) is milder and appears briefly at Asian markets.
seasons.storageTip: Unpeeled in the fridge for weeks. Freeze whole — grate directly from frozen.

seasons.notes: Always imported. No meaningful seasonal quality variation.
seasons.storageTip: Unpeeled in the fridge for weeks. Freeze whole — grate directly from frozen.
